description: scholar-slides regression benchmark — real papers built end-to-end. Every code change is re-run through run_benchmark.mjs (integrity QA gate + PPTX content parity + aesthetic layout-mix) across all entries, so a fix that helps one deck cannot silently regress another. Only deck.json (the durable spec) is committed; the built deck/ dir AND the figure crops under out/<name>/figures/ are build products, git-ignored, and absent on a clean clone. To (re)build a deck: (Stage 1) `python scripts/prepare_source.py <pdf|arXiv-id> --out out/<name>` regenerates the figure crops from the source PDF — note some crops are hand-adjusted (see per-deck notes) and won't reproduce byte-for-byte; then (Stage 2/5) `node scripts/build_deck.mjs out/<name>/deck.json && node scripts/render_deck.mjs out/<name>/deck/deck.html pdf && node scripts/export_pptx.mjs out/<name>/deck.json out/<name>/deck/deck.pptx`. When a figure crop is absent, the deck still builds and the PPTX-parity gate degrades that figure to a WARN skip rather than a hard failure, so the benchmark is reproducible on a clean clone. run_benchmark reports unbuilt decks as NOT-BUILT (non-fatal). Grow toward 15+ cross-discipline papers (CV / NLP / medical / theory / biology).
